description: POSE UI/UX one-shot evaluation suite. Tests whether a model can produce shippable frontend output — a SaaS hero section and a Snake game — from a single prompt. Outputs are self-contained HTML files evaluated for completeness, correctness, and visual quality.

test_cases.name: SaaS Hero Section
test_cases.prompt:
Build a modern SaaS landing page hero section as a single self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. No external libraries or frameworks.

### Design Requirements

**Layout (top to bottom):**
1. **Navigation bar** — Logo on the left ("Flowly"), nav links in the center (Features, Pricing, Docs, Blog), and a "Get started free" CTA button on the right. Sticky on scroll.
2. **Hero content** — Centered, two-column layout:
   - Left: Headline, subheadline, two CTA buttons, and social proof
   - Right: Animated product UI mockup (browser chrome + dashboard screenshot using pure CSS/div elements — no images)
3. **Social proof strip** — Logos of "trusted by" companies rendered as styled text badges (e.g., Stripe, Notion, Linear, Vercel, Figma) in a muted row beneath the hero

**Copy:**
- Headline: "Ship faster with AI-powered workflows" (bold, large, gradient text)
- Subheadline: "Flowly connects your tools, automates your busywork, and keeps your team in sync — so you can focus on what matters."
- Primary CTA: "Start for free →" (filled button)
- Secondary CTA: "Watch demo" (ghost button with play icon)
- Social proof text: "Loved by 12,000+ teams worldwide ★★★★★"

**Visual Style:**
- Dark background: `#0a0a0f`
- Accent gradient: `#7c3aed` → `#2563eb` (purple to blue)
- Headline gradient text using the accent gradient
- Card/surface color: `#12121a`
- Border color: `rgba(255,255,255,0.08)`
- Body text: `#94a3b8`
- White text for headings
- Font: system-ui stack (no external fonts)

**Product mockup (right column):**
- Browser chrome (three colored dots, URL bar) in a dark rounded card
- Inside: A simplified dashboard UI made of divs:
  - A sidebar with colored icon blocks and text stubs
  - A main area with a fake bar chart (5 bars of varying heights using divs)
  - Two stat cards at the top right showing "Tasks Done: 1,284" and "Time Saved: 47h"
- The whole card should have a subtle purple glow (box-shadow)
- Animate the card with a slow float up/down animation (3s ease-in-out infinite)

**Navigation behavior:**
- On scroll past 80px, the navbar adds a backdrop blur and border-bottom
- "Get started free" button uses the accent gradient as background

**Animations:**
- Headline words fade in with a stagger (0.1s delay per word)
- CTA buttons have a hover lift effect (translateY(-2px))
- Mockup card floats (keyframe animation)

**Responsive:**
- At < 768px, switch to single-column (content stacked above mockup)
- Nav links collapse (just show logo + CTA button at mobile)
test_cases.reference_answer: A complete self-contained HTML file that: renders a sticky dark navbar with logo and CTA; shows a two-column hero with gradient headline text, two CTA buttons, and a social proof strip; includes a CSS-only product mockup (browser chrome with a fake dashboard) with a float animation; collapses to single column on mobile; uses no external libraries.

scoring_criteria.completeness: All major sections are present: navbar, two-column hero, product mockup, social proof strip. Both CTA buttons exist and are visually distinct.
scoring_criteria.accuracy: Gradient text is implemented correctly (background-clip: text). The mockup is built from CSS/HTML divs only — no img tags. The floating animation uses a CSS keyframe.
scoring_criteria.format: Valid, self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JS. No external stylesheet or framework CDN links. File opens in a browser without errors.
scoring_criteria.domain_relevance: The output looks like a real SaaS product landing page — dark theme, clear value proposition, professional visual hierarchy.
scoring_criteria.clarity: Code is readable and structured — not a single minified blob. CSS and JS are organized in their respective style and script tags.

test_cases.name: Snake Game
test_cases.prompt:
Build a fully playable Snake game as a single self-contained HTML file with embedded CSS and JavaScript. No external libraries.

### Game Specifications

**Canvas:**
- Size: 600×600 pixels
- Grid: 20×20 cells (each cell is 30×30 pixels)
- Background: `#0a0a0f` (very dark)
- Grid lines: subtle `rgba(255,255,255,0.03)` to give a faint grid texture

**Snake:**
- Head: `#7c3aed` (purple)
- Body segments: gradient from `#5b21b6` (near head) to `#2563eb` (near tail)
- Rounded corners on each segment

**Food:**
- A pulsing red circle (`#ef4444`) centered in a random empty cell

**Game loop:**
- Target: 10 moves per second (100ms tick interval)
- Arrow keys control direction
- Snake cannot reverse direction

**Collision:**
- Wall collision: snake hits canvas edge → game over
- Self collision: snake head overlaps any body segment → game over

**Scoring:**
- Eating food: +10 points
- Snake grows by 1 segment when food is eaten
- High score persists in `localStorage` across page refreshes

**UI (outside the canvas):**
- Title: "🐍 Snake" above the canvas
- Score display: "Score: 0" and "Best: 0" updated in real time
- Start screen overlay: "Press Space or Enter to start"
- Game over overlay: "Game Over" + final score + "Press Space or Enter to restart"

**Speed increase:**
- Every 5 foods eaten, speed increases slightly (minimum 50ms tick)

**States:** IDLE → PLAYING → GAME_OVER
test_cases.reference_answer: A complete self-contained HTML file containing a playable Snake game. The snake moves on a 600x600 canvas, eats food to grow, dies on wall or self-collision, shows score and best score, persists high score in localStorage, has start and game-over states with keyboard controls, and increases speed as the score grows.

scoring_criteria.completeness: All core game features are implemented: snake movement, food spawning, eating/growing, wall collision, self-collision, score tracking, high score in localStorage, start/game-over states, speed progression.
scoring_criteria.accuracy: Game mechanics are correct: snake cannot reverse direction, food never spawns on the snake body, self-collision is detected accurately, score increments by exactly 10 per food.
scoring_criteria.format: Valid self-contained HTML file. Game runs without errors in a browser. No external libraries. Canvas is present and used for rendering.
scoring_criteria.domain_relevance: The output is a recognizable, playable Snake game — not just a canvas with random movement. The core loop (move, eat, grow, die) works as expected.
scoring_criteria.clarity: Game state machine is clear. Key functions (update, draw, handleInput) are identifiable. Code does not produce console errors during normal gameplay.
